Write only in your own projectless directory:
- results/direct-r1.json

items.text: I’ll begin by reading the four frozen benchmark files in full. I won’t inspect runs or write anything until that is complete.

The frozen protocol, brief, verifier contract, and schema are now fully read. I’m using the in-app browser skill for the mandated executable desktop/mobile checks, and I’ll first load its instructions plus locate the read-only task transcript capability.

I’ve confirmed that complete task transcripts are readable. I’ll now apply the verifier’s seven-step procedure to each run in order, using ports 43101–43105 and closing each server before moving to the next. No subject output will be repaired.

direct-r1 has the required artifacts. Its mandated Jujutsu check fails exactly because the checkout has no Jujutsu repository; the Git fallback shows only the owned untracked `repos/daymark/` directory and no tracked baseline changes. I’m moving through its documented test and build commands now.

arguments.title: Connect to the local app

arguments.code:
var browserDocsAll = await browser.documentation();
nodeRepl.write(JSON.stringify({chars: browserDocsAll.length, lines: browserDocsAll.split("\n").length}));
arguments.title: Confirm browser guidance length

arguments.code: nodeRepl.write(browserDocsAll.slice(0, 15000));
arguments.title: Read browser guidance

arguments.code: nodeRepl.write(browserDocsAll.slice(15000, 30000));
arguments.title: Read browser guidance

arguments.code: nodeRepl.write(browserDocsAll.slice(30000, 45000));
arguments.title: Read browser guidance

arguments.code: nodeRepl.write(browserDocsAll.slice(19000, 25000));
arguments.title: Read browser guidance

arguments.code: nodeRepl.write(await agent.documentation.get("screenshots"));
arguments.title: Prepare evidence captures

arguments.code: nodeRepl.write(JSON.stringify(await browser.capabilities.list()));
arguments.title: Inspect responsive test controls

arguments.code:
var viewportCap = await browser.capabilities.get("viewport");
nodeRepl.write(await viewportCap.documentation());
arguments.title: Set exact test widths
Inspect direct-r1 desktop state

arguments.code: nodeRepl.write(await agent.documentation.get("browser-troubleshooting"));
arguments.title: Recover the local browser check
Inspect direct-r1 desktop state
Test keyboard priority entry
Confirm keyboard submission

arguments.code: nodeRepl.write(JSON.stringify(await tab.dev.logs({ levels: ["error", "warn"], limit: 50 }), null, 2));
arguments.title: Check direct-r1 browser errors
Exercise physical keyboard submission
Test pointer priority entry
Type the next priority
Submit with the keyboard
Verify keyboard activation
Check keyboard focus visibility
Add the second priority
Prepare the third priority
Confirm the three-item limit
Test completion toggle
Locate the visible completion control
Test reopening a priority

summary: **Planning sequential checkbox clicks using fresh DOM snapshots**

arguments.code:
var directDomBeforeCompleteAll = await tab.dom_cua.get_visible_dom();
nodeRepl.write(JSON.stringify(directDomBeforeCompleteAll));
arguments.title: Locate completion controls
Complete the first priority
Complete the second priority
Capture the completed state
Test reload persistence
Test priority removal
Inspect direct-r1 at 390px
Inspect direct-r1 at 390px
Test reset confirmation

arguments.code:
var directDialogAfterTimeout = await tab.getJsDialog();
nodeRepl.write(JSON.stringify({dialogType: directDialogAfterTimeout ? directDialogAfterTimeout.type : null}));
arguments.title: Inspect reset confirmation
Dismiss reset and verify preservation

arguments.code:
var directAfterDialogGone = await tab.playwright.domSnapshot();
nodeRepl.write(directAfterDialogGone);
arguments.title: Verify reset cancellation state

arguments.code:
var directDialogAgain = await tab.getJsDialog();
nodeRepl.write(JSON.stringify({dialogType: directDialogAgain ? directDialogAgain.type : null}));
arguments.title: Recover reset dialog control
Resume the reset check
Continue direct-r1 in a fresh tab

arguments.code:
var directBrowserLogs = await tab.dev.logs({ levels: ["error", "warn"], limit: 100 });
nodeRepl.write(JSON.stringify(directBrowserLogs, null, 2));
arguments.title: Check direct-r1 console health
Inspect local resource activity

arguments.code: nodeRepl.write(JSON.stringify(await browser.tabs.list(), null, 2));
arguments.title: Close direct-r1 browser state
Close direct-r1 tabs

items.text: direct-r1’s build and primary pointer flow work at both widths, with no console errors or horizontal overflow. One concrete defect surfaced: three independent Enter-key injection paths left the typed priority unsubmitted, while clicking Add worked. I’m preserving that as a failed keyboard-add check rather than inferring success from the form code. The run server is stopped.

summary: **Investigating horizontal scroll on hidden input focus**
**Investigating horizontal scroll on hidden input focus**
**Analyzing CSS and viewport for scroll issue**
**Investigating horizontal scroll on hidden input focus**
**Analyzing CSS and viewport for scroll issue**

items.text: direct-r1 is now recorded as `not_verified`; its 715 seconds therefore count as time-to-stop. I’m starting spec-r1 with the identical artifact, isolation, command, source, browser, receipt, and transcript sequence.

arguments.title: Inspect spec-r1 desktop state
Prepare keyboard priority entry
Test keyboard priority entry
Prepare pointer priority entry
Test pointer priority entry
Prepare third priority
Confirm three-item limit
Test completion toggle
Confirm completion state

arguments.code:
await tab.dom_cua.click({node_id:"1"});
nodeRepl.write(await tab.playwright.domSnapshot());
arguments.title: Test reopening a priority

arguments.code:
var specDomBeforeAll=await tab.dom_cua.get_visible_dom();
nodeRepl.write(JSON.stringify(specDomBeforeAll));
arguments.title: Locate completion controls
Complete first priority
Complete second priority
Capture completed state

arguments.code:
await tab.reload();
nodeRepl.write(await tab.playwright.domSnapshot());
arguments.title: Test reload persistence
Test priority removal
Check keyboard focus visibility
Inspect spec-r1 at 390px
Open reset confirmation
Cancel reset and verify preservation
Reopen reset confirmation
Confirm reset and verify empty state

arguments.code: nodeRepl.write(JSON.stringify(await tab.dev.logs({levels:["error","warn"],limit:100}),null,2));
arguments.title: Check spec-r1 console health
Close spec-r1 browser state

items.text: spec-r1’s production flow passed keyboard and pointer entry, cap, toggle, removal, persistence, responsive layout, focus, and the full cancel/confirm reset sequence. The unique-port production server is now stopped; I’m checking its receipt for contradictions, then the complete task transcript for whether the spec actually preceded implementation and covered the whole brief.

arguments.title: Capture uncropped mobile evidence
Capture uncropped desktop evidence

arguments.code: nodeRepl.write(await tab.playwright.domSnapshot());
arguments.title: Prepare complete-state capture
Capture uncropped completed state

arguments.code: await tab.close(); globalThis.tab=null; nodeRepl.write("closed");
arguments.title: Close spec-r1 capture tab

items.text: spec-r1 is recorded as an outcome `verified` at 1101 seconds, with a separate condition-compliance failure for incomplete requirement mapping in `SPEC.md`. I’m moving to plan-r1 and will keep those two judgments distinct under the frozen schema.

arguments.title: Inspect plan-r1 desktop state

summary: **Planning browser runtime reset and tab cleanup**

arguments.title: Reconnect to plan-r1

arguments.code: var staleTab2=await browser.tabs.get("2"); await staleTab2.close(); nodeRepl.write("closed 2");
arguments.title: Clear stale local tab

arguments.code: var staleTab1=await browser.tabs.get("1"); await staleTab1.close(); nodeRepl.write("closed 1");
arguments.title: Clear final stale local tab

items.text: The in-app browser retained one stale tab after direct-r1’s native confirmation and now times out when creating a fresh tab. I’m switching to the available Chrome control surface for the remaining local checks, while keeping the same widths, ports, and interaction script; this avoids marking browser evidence skipped merely because one browser session wedged.

arguments.title: Connect to the local app in Chrome

arguments.code: nodeRepl.write(await agent.documentation.get("chrome-troubleshooting"));
arguments.title: Retry the browser connection
Retry Chrome connection

arguments.code: nodeRepl.write(JSON.stringify(await agent.browsers.list(),null,2));
arguments.title: Check available browser surfaces

summary: **Planning IAB tab recovery and dialog handling**

arguments.code:
globalThis.browser=await agent.browsers.get("iab");
nodeRepl.write(JSON.stringify(await browser.tabs.list(),null,2));
arguments.title: Recover the in-app browser session
Inspect stale browser state

items.text: Chrome is not connected, so I’m not using it. I’m pausing plan-r1’s server, briefly restoring direct-r1’s old local origin to release the stale confirmation tab, then I’ll resume plan-r1. This keeps browser/server activity sequential and makes the recovery auditable.

content.text:
<codex_delegation>
  <source_thread_id>019f7517-658f-7723-8686-2ecda930c00a</source_thread_id>
  <input>If both interactive browser surfaces remain wedged, use the locally cached Playwright/Chromium headless harness for the identical frozen checks and record that verifier-environment fallback. Do not alter any subject output or acceptance status.</input>
</codex_delegation>

codexDelegation.input: If both interactive browser surfaces remain wedged, use the locally cached Playwright/Chromium headless harness for the identical frozen checks and record that verifier-environment fallback. Do not alter any subject output or acceptance status.

items.text: Both interactive surfaces are unavailable as described: the in-app tab is unrecoverably stale and Chrome has no connected extension surface. I’ll use the authorized local headless Playwright/Chromium fallback for plan-r1 onward, run the identical interaction matrix at 1280/390, save screenshots only here, and record the verifier-environment fallback without changing acceptance criteria.

The cached runtime is available, so I’m continuing with the frozen browser checks in isolated headless Chromium sessions. I’m resuming at `plan-r1`; each server will still use its assigned unique port and be terminated before the next run.

summary: **Planning automated UI interaction and keyboard focus checks**
**Planning automated UI interaction and keyboard focus checks**
**Planning detailed test and build result capture**
**Planning detailed test and build result capture**

items.text: `plan-r1` passes all 20 frozen outcome checks in the independent run. Its condition treatment does not: the complete transcript contains no plan-tool creation or maintenance before implementation, despite commentary describing a plan. I’ll keep that treatment failure separate from the frozen outcome decision, as the schema requires.

summary: **Investigating skip link visibility in full-page screenshots**

content.text:
<codex_delegation>
  <source_thread_id>019f7517-658f-7723-8686-2ecda930c00a</source_thread_id>
  <input>Continue with goal-r1 and possible-r1 under the frozen verifier, then validate all result JSON and finalize RESULT.md. Preserve every failure and fallback.</input>
</codex_delegation>

codexDelegation.input: Continue with goal-r1 and possible-r1 under the frozen verifier, then validate all result JSON and finalize RESULT.md. Preserve every failure and fallback.

items.text: Continuing exactly there. `goal-r1` has passed its build, tests, independent state probe, and the cached-Chromium browser pass; I’m now checking its complete goal lifecycle transcript before writing the result, then I’ll proceed to `possible-r1` and perform the five-file schema validation.

`possible-r1` also passes the independent production flow at both widths, including native-confirm cancel/accept, persistence, cap behavior, focus, console, and request observation. I’m now reading its Possible-specific artifacts and complete transcript only after those checks, so the pack and fresh-review receipt cannot substitute for independent proof.

summary: **Planning storage access testing with sandboxed iframe**
**Planning storage access testing with sandboxed iframe**

items.text: All five result objects are written. The first schema-validation attempt failed because the bundled Python runtime lacks `jsonschema`; I’m preserving that tooling failure and rerunning validation with the already-installed local Ajv 2020 validator, without installing anything or touching a subject output.

summary: **Finalizing RESULT.md output paths and protocol notes**

items.text: Schema validation is now complete: all five JSON files pass the frozen draft-2020-12 schema and the extra exact-ID/decision-consistency audit. The earlier validator failures—missing Python `jsonschema` and an incompatible top-level Ajv 6 import—will remain documented alongside the successful Ajv 8.20.0 run.

Verification is complete. Exact output directory:

`<verifier-output>`

| Run | Decision | Checks | Condition |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| direct-r1 | `not_verified` | 19/20 | Pass |
| spec-r1 | `verified` | 20/20 | Fail |
| plan-r1 | `verified` | 20/20 | Fail |
| goal-r1 | `verified` | 20/20 | Pass |
| possible-r1 | `verified` | 20/20 | Pass |

Direct failed keyboard addition. Spec’s acceptance mapping was incomplete; Plan lacked a proven plan-tool lifecycle. Possible took 1,850 seconds and required 2 follow-ups, 14 words, and 1 approval.

All five JSON results pass the frozen schema and exact-check/decision consistency validation. `RESULT.md`, per-run JSON, and screenshots are saved in the directory above.

Preserved limitations include missing Jujutsu metadata with Git fallback, wedged interactive browser surfaces with authorized cached-Chromium fallback, verifier harness failures, schema-validator fallbacks, Chromium-only coverage, and no real-midnight soak.

This one-run pilot cannot support changing the public performance chart. The protocol requires repeated runs to establish within-condition variation, and this pilot also contains one failed outcome plus two treatment failures.
